Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: White diamond at (20, 13) area (top-left). Orange ring still orange at bottom. Purple square at (41, 49). Purple ring at top-right. Board changed after clicking (50,37) but orange ring unchanged.
Goal model: Convert orange ring to purple. Need to find the right sequence.
Recent findings: Clicking orange ring pixels doesn't convert them directly. May need white diamond at specific position first.
Plan: Try clicking purple square (41,49) to move white diamond there, then click orange ring pixels to test conversion.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking purple square to move white diamond there
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 49}])

# Check the result
print(f"Game state: {last_action_result.get('state', 'N/A')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', 'N/A')}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', 'N/A')}")

# Check current white diamond position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find white objects with 5 pixels
white_5 = []
for node in nodes:
    if node['color'] == 'w' and node['pixels'] == 5:
        if node['boundary']:
            center_row = sum(p[0] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
            center_col = sum(p[1] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
            white_5.append((center_row, center_col))

print(f"White 5-pixel objects: {white_5}")

# Check orange ring area
orange_count = 0
purple_count = 0
for node in nodes:
    if node['boundary']:
        for p in node['boundary']:
            row, col = p
            if 45 <= row <= 55 and 35 <= col <= 45:
                if node['color'] == 'O':
                    orange_count += 1
                elif node['color'] == 'p':
                    purple_count += 1

print(f"Orange pixels in ring area: {orange_count}")
print(f"Purple pixels in ring area: {purple_count}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
